segments.text: Okay, I'll talk about multilingual NLP.
And multilingual NLP is NLP in many different languages.
There is specifically two varieties of multilingual NLP.
The first one is monolingual NLP in multiple languages.
And what I mean by this is basically any task that you could do in English.
You could do it in languages that are not English.
And so this would be a question answering sentiment analysis,
chatbots, code generation, whatever else.
And then the other one is some variety of cross,
cross-lingual NLP.
And this is specifically test set, handle more than one language at once.
And so that would be things like machine translation, cross-lingual QA, etc, etc.
Cross-lingual QA is just like, for example,
answering questions where the source materials in a different language.
So if I ask a question in Japanese,
it can go find some information in English and answer the question in Japanese.
So right now, so many of our systems are trained by,
you know, using large data sets.

a positive data in many of the languages that we care about.

but it's not quite as sharp in general text on the internet.

but it's not quite as bad as in wiki pdia.

of course, because the annotated data is a subset of monolingual data.

So, how do we start attacking multilingual problems? So like one really huge thing over the past five years or seven years or so.
Is that, um, we can learn models that process multiple languages and all the languages can learn from each other and that really pulls up the languages that don't have a lot of data.
And, um, so this is a trans, a variety of transfer learning.
And this allows you to improve accuracy on lower resource languages by leveraging data and higher resource languages.

Um, another really big advantage of multilingual learning and learning models that work in multiple languages is practical, which is before Google translate would deploy 100 models or maybe even 200 models because if they were translating into English and then out of English, they would have one model for like English to Chinese English to Japanese English to French English to Spanish and then Spanish to English French to English to English.
And so they would have to deal with like deploying all of these models, having different servers that served all of them and stuff like this.

Now you can just have one big model that handles all of the languages at once and to play it, which allows you to make that model bigger itself because you need to deploy it fewer times and also, you know, it can benefit from transfer learning.
So because of this, a lot of places that handle multilingual stuff are trans like changing to this paradigm.
